title: The World Without Humans
transcript_text: you know what would happen if all humans suddenly disappeared from the planet after about 1 week all of the lights would turn off because no one was there to man the power grids after about 2 weeks all of the house pets still trapped inside would pass away due to dehydration or starvation while the rest took to the streets and search for food after 1 month all the nuclear power plants would become unstable leading to nuclear meltdowns across the entire planet after about 1 year any City not affected by nuclear fallout would be completely reclaimed by Mother Nature and all of the satellites would start to fall back down to earth after 200 years all of our largest buildings and structures would begin to collapse within 35,000 years the plant cycle removes all traces of lead left from the Industrial Revolution from Earth's soil 65,000 years after that CO2 levels in our atmosphere return to prehuman levels Mount Rushmore survives for 7.2 million years before finally caving in and the last traces of humanity will be our Bronze Statues and cities buried underground like some sort of techn fossil

title: The Greatest Shot in Television History
transcript_text: they say this is the single greatest shot in all of television history and I want you to tell me if you agree or not top off the flask the gases evaporate you apply light and boom now two gases do that better than any other and it was alert's assistant who put them together most efficiently his name was Verna Von Brown and the two gases that he released from his particular version of a thermos flask the one lying on its side behind me now were hydrogen and oxygen if you release those two gas passes into a confined space with a hole at the other end of it and mix them as you do so and then set light to them you get that destination the moon or Moscow the planets or ping

title: Can You Drink Glacier Water?
transcript_text: Please don't ever do this. There seems to be this trend online of people going to glaciers, specifically in Alaska, and drinking the water. And just because it looks like blue Gatorade doesn't necessarily mean that it's safe to drink. On one hand, you have tons of people saying that glacier water is full of ancient bacteria, animal feces, ice worms, and more. And under no circumstances should you ever drink it. Then you have people like Ludicrous who went and drank from a glacier in Alaska and said it felt like every single one of the cells in his body was being hydrated at once. Oh my god. Oh. Professor Martin Truffer, who studies glaciers for a living at the University of Alaska, says that glacier water is extremely clean and doesn't really pose a health concern. But official government health websites do not recommend drinking raw glacier water and say it should always be treated before consumption.

title: SPACE PHOTOGRAPHY
transcript_text: I hate to be the one to say it but the universe doesn't actually look like this or this or this even no one is going to argue with you that space photography has come a long way in the last 80 years the first photo ever taken in space was this in 1946 at 65 Mi up just above the beginning of outer space now we get pictures like this almost monthly it seems showing us these beautiful colorful images of the universe around us but there's one problem when satellites like the James web telescope are taking these pictures they're capturing light across different wavelengths including infrared ultraviolet or x-ray which aren't visible to the human eye NASA will then go in assigning color to these different wavelengths to highlight specific features and structures for the viewer this process known as false coloring allows them to reveal details in space that would otherwise be invisible if you were actually up there

title: The Last Person on Earth
transcript_text: let's say you wake up tomorrow and you can't find anyone and I mean anyone every single person on Earth has disappeared except for you what do you do well you have about 1 week left of electricity before all of the lights turn off for the very last time because there was no one there to man the power grids all of the house pets on earth have now been trapped inside of their homes so try and free as many of them as possible and maybe pick up a companion or two those you can't free have about 2 weeks before they pass away due to dehydration or starvation eat as many imported Foods as you can before they expire like bananas pineapple and avocado because once they're gone you're going to have a very hard time finding more then try and figure out how close you are to the nearest nuclear reactor because after about a month they're going to become unstable and start having meltdowns you have about 2 years before all of the fuel expires so I would recommend getting in a car and driving to wherever you want to set yourself up for the long term after about a year the cities will start to be reclaimed by Nature making your surroundings a lot Greener watch your head because it's around this time where satellites will start falling back to Earth

title: Universe 25
transcript_text: Universe 25 might be the scariest experiment of all time in the 1960s a lot of people became concerned about what would happen if the global population exceeded global resources something that we still struggle with today but one guy John B Calhoun had the opposite concern and wanted to know what would happen to a society with too much abundance so he put it to the test he took four breeding pairs of mice and put them in an enclosure with enough space and enough food for 5,000 mice his question was what happens to mice when they don't have to worry about food temperature space shelter or disease and essentially live in a mice Utopia and the answer was they got busy reproducing after 145 days the population skyrocketed from 8 to 620 with nothing really to do the mice started to separate themselves into different social groups and the mice that couldn't find a role in a group withdrew themselves and started to Pile in the middle the groups then started to turn on and eat each other mothers simply forgot about or abandoned their young most mice eventually gave up mating altogether with the low birth rate high infant mortality and even higher violence the entire Colony went extinct shortly after the population peaked at 2,200 in an enclosure with enough space and enough food for 5,000
